rationale_for_baseline_tier: A benign but symptomatic papulopustular rosacea flare — prompt prescription and skincare/trigger advice with a clear safety-net for facial cellulitis and ocular rosacea; not an emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Face becomes hot, hard, swollen with a fever, or redness spreads (esp. toward the eyelids) with a sharp advancing border
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests facial/periorbital cellulitis needing antibiotics/urgent assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your face becomes hot, hard and swollen with a fever, or the redness spreads (especially toward your eyelids) — seek urgent medical care/ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Red, painful or gritty eyes, or blurred vision
escalation_edges.rationale: Ocular rosacea can threaten the cornea.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your eyes become red, painful or gritty, or your vision blurs, get a prompt eye check.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Acknowledge distress; set realistic timelines and reassure about the benign nature.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a rosacea flare, not an infection. Start the low-dose anti-inflammatory doxycycline, stop harsh scrubs, use gentle skincare and sun protection, avoid triggers, and use cool compresses. Watch for a hot/hard swollen face with fever, or red/painful/gritty eyes.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription/review; urgent care/ED for cellulitis; eye review for ocular symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; ED for facial cellulitis; eye care for ocular symptoms.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document papulopustular rosacea flare (cellulitis/ocular rosacea screened negative), anti-inflammatory therapy, skincare/trigger advice.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassurance with no cellulitis/ocular screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An actual facial/periorbital cellulitis or ocular rosacea is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without treatment or skincare/trigger advice.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency escalation for a benign symmetrical rosacea flare without red flags.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate for induration or examine the eyes with a slit-lamp remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as papulopustular rosacea with anti-inflammatory therapy and skincare/trigger advice, and give a facial-cellulitis and ocular-rosacea safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Treating as cellulitis/acne, or missing ocular rosacea.
